title: NIS2 Human Resources Security — Joiner/Mover/Leaver Process
exclusions: Does not execute HR disciplinary processes, notify labor councils, decide rehire eligibility, or replace court-grade HRIS extracts when employment law mandates certified paper files.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.14` with hashed identifiers, companion `hr:jml_events` excerpts proving termination_date provenance, ServiceNow RITM closure IDs, and TSA countersign files per the corporate evidence-signing standard for timestamped archives.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(i) requires human resources security. The UC monitors joiner-mover-leaver process telemetry from the HRIS and IdP to flag delayed deprovisions and orphaned accounts. It cannot verify that background checks were completed or that HR policies meet legal requirements, hence partial.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) (Human resources and access control) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.14: NIS2 Human Resources Security — Joiner/Mover/Leaver Process.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.14' running on sourcetype WinEventLog:Security and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Controlled test user `jdoe@corp` receives `termination_date=yesterday` in `terminated_employees.csv` while a scripted Entra sign-in occurs today; the join surfaces `days_active_after_term=1` and nonzero `auth_count`, demonstrating detection of lingering access.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same user after Global Admin runs `Revoke sessions` shows no `azure:aad:signin` or Windows success events for thirty days, HR adds `event_type=rehire_cancelled` row, and the search returns empty while HRIS attestation PDF is uploaded manually for auditors.

dataSources: `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:jml_events` via HEC (fields employee_id, event_type, termination_date, job_change_id); `index=auth` `sourcetype=azure:aad:signin` with Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services ([Splunkbase 3110](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110)); `index=windows` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`; `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:task` (deprovision tickets) via Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)).
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110)
spl:
| inputlookup terminated_employees.csv
| eval term_date=strptime(termination_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_since_term=round((now()-term_date)/86400, 0)
| join type=left user [
    search index=auth OR index=windows OR index=azure earliest=-30d
    | eval user=coalesce(Account_Name, userPrincipalName, user)
    | stats latest(_time) as last_auth count as auth_count by user
]
| where isnotnull(last_auth) AND last_auth > term_date
| eval days_active_after_term=round((last_auth - term_date)/86400, 0)
| sort - days_active_after_term
| table user, termination_date, days_since_term, days_active_after_term, auth_count
description: Joins authoritative HR termination dates from `terminated_employees.csv` (or HEC `hr:jml_events`) against subsequent authentication activity drawn from Entra ID, Windows, and shared auth indexes to spotlight accounts still authenticating after the leaver timestamp.
value: Provisioning audits rarely fail on paper—they fail when old accounts keep logging in because a deletion script missed a shadow admin; giving the privacy officer and risk committee a dated trail of post-termination authentications closes that evidentiary gap ahead of regulator questions.
implementation: (1) HR publishes `terminated_employees.csv` nightly with `user` UPN + `termination_date`. (2) Map `index=hr` feed for near-real-time events. (3) Alert if `days_active_after_term>0`. (4) Severity++ if >3 days. (5) Auto-task ServiceNow `RITM` via alert. (6) Store signed exports with `evidenceSigning`.
visualization: Timeline of last_auth vs term_date, gauge of open violations, ServiceNow ticket drilldown.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user
dataModelAcceleration: Enable for Authentication (optional acceleration for 30d windows)
knownFalsePositives: 1) **Shared mailbox or role account renamed** so HR CSV still lists old `user` string while authentication uses new alias. *Distinguish:* Azure `userPrincipalName` change logs in **Entra ID > Audit**. *Suppress:* lookup `nis2_account_alias_map.csv`. 2) **Contractor extensions** recorded late in HRIS. *Distinguish:* `event_type=rehire_pending` in `hr:jml_events`. *Suppress:* time-bound exception register until HR closes ticket. 3) **Offline laptop logging cached credentials** days after term but never hits network until imaging. *Distinguish:* `last_auth` older than 72h yet after term. *Suppress:* optional `where days_active_after_term>3` for paging only. 4) **ServiceNow deprovision automation re-enabling SaaS sandbox accounts** for knowledge transfer. *Distinguish:* `RITM` contains `re_enable_for_kt`. *Suppress:* `lookup nis2_kt_exceptions user`. 5) **Timezone-aligned termination at midnight UTC vs local HR calendar**. *Distinguish:* HR provides `termination_timezone`. *Suppress:* adjust `term_date` with `strptime` + offset.
equipmentModels: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) sign-in logs via Graph API
Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110, Entra sign-in ingestion)
Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742, DC Security EventLog)
Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, RITM deprovision tickets)
Workday / SAP SuccessFactors / BambooHR (HRIS termination feeds)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Indexes**: `auth`, `windows`, `azure` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `terminated_employees.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('terminated_employees.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.21(2)(i)`. The control objective is: "Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(i) (Human resources and access control) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.14: NIS2 Human Resources Security — Joiner/Mover/Leaver Process." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `auth`, `windows`, `azure` under the relevant sourcetypes, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


6 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** HR publishes `terminated_employees.csv` nightly with `user` UPN + `termination_date`. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 2:** Map `index=hr` feed for near-real-time events. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** Alert if `days_active_after_term>0`. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** Severity++ if >3 days. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 5:** Auto-task ServiceNow `RITM` via alert. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 6:** Store signed exports with `evidenceSigning`. Report-generation cadence must match the regulatory reporting cycle; a monthly digest satisfies an annual review requirement but not a quarterly one.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Human Resources Security — Joiner/Mover/Leaver Process," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the NIS2 control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search correlates `index=auth` (sourcetype=`the primary`) on the outer side with `index=windows` (sourcetype=`another`) on the inner side, joined on `user` across the last 30 days. `type=left` preserves all rows from the outer search; nulls in the inner side indicate missing data that may itself constitute a finding.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

Time-zone sensitivity: the `strptime(termination_date, "%Y-%m-%d")` conversion assumes the source timestamp is in the Splunk server's local time zone unless the format includes `%z`. If `termination_date` is emitted in UTC by the source system but Splunk's search head runs in a non-UTC zone, the resulting epoch will be off by the zone offset and the subsequent `where _time > termination_date_epoch` comparison will produce systematic false positives or negatives. Always validate by comparing `strftime(termination_date_epoch, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")` against a known-good reference event before promoting to production.

**CIM / accelerated variant:** The detection also has an accelerated variant using `| tstats` against the CIM `Authentication` datamodel:

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user
```

When to prefer this variant: once the underlying datamodel is accelerated (verify in Settings > Data Models > `Authentication` > status=100%), this query runs 10-50x faster than the raw variant and can feasibly cover multi-month windows for audit reporting. When to avoid: if your field extractions are app-specific and the CIM tagging is incomplete, the `tstats` variant will silently under-count. Always compare raw vs accelerated counts on a known day before swapping the production schedule. Maintain both variants in the UC — the raw is the primary for accuracy, the accelerated is the performance optimisation for dashboarding.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.14: NIS2 Human Resources Security — Joiner/Mover/Leaver Process]
cron_schedule = 0 3 1 * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -30d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.14,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=auth sourcetype=the_sourcetype earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `auth_count`, `days_active_after_term`, `days_since_term`, `last_auth`, `term_date`, `termination_date`, `user`. Run:

```spl
index=auth sourcetype=the_sourcetype earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table auth_count, days_active_after_term, days_since_term, last_auth, term_date, termination_date, user
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.14: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Timeline Of Last Auth Vs Term Date (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Gauge Of Open Violations (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Servicenow Ticket Drilldown (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: When someone stops working for us, we check the electronic keys still are not used afterward. If a former employee’s name still shows up logging in after their last day, we treat that as urgent housekeeping.
